notes: THE CATALOG — the alternate-universe slowstick lattice made visible (liner notes). Studios, cities, years, credits from lore/_SLOWSTICK_CATALOG_ROADMAP.md + _SLOWSTICK_AESTHETIC_BIBLE.md — the canon that must not be contradicted. `played` is an AlmanacState predicate (token/token_prefix/any) marking whether the shelf has seen this cart. Grouped by studio in the collector view; achievements in catalog_achievements below.

sagebrush.name: Sagebrush Engineworks
sagebrush.note: Two people, a programmer and a paperback-cover painter. Folded by 1986. Never answered a letter.

astro_cortex.name: Astro-Cortex Software
astro_cortex.note: Precision-instrument glass. Where the 220 Hz channel and the Corrections were born.

oneironautics.name: Oneironautics Inc.
oneironautics.note: Field-guide gouache. The house the Rochas built, 1988 to 2005.

pdp_toys.name: PDP Toys
pdp_toys.note: Injection-molded toy-bright. Made the Riffmaster kids' synth.

yumemi.name: Yumemi Denshi
yumemi.note: Inked line on paper tones, a hanging scroll's patience. Founded by Sachiko Ono.

homebrew.name: no publisher
homebrew.note: One ink, one duplicator, dialed to near-nothing. Made by a survivor's kid.

ranch.name: RANCH
ranch.note: Laminate-and-signage commercial graphics. Two ex-Oneironautics designers who left in 2000 to make the manager-sim commercial.

meridian.name: Meridian Heritage Interactive
meridian.note: Licensed the Oneironautics catalog in 2041. The remake that proves the original mattered.

carts.title: The Sisters Wyrd
carts.blurb: The oldest playable cart on the shelf. Pulp-paperback inks, one violet held in reserve for when the field goes thin.

carts.title: The Earthman Chronicles
carts.credits: sr. eng. J.F.; QA A. (Amélie) Rocha
carts.blurb: Jack Parsons, John Carter, and Hubbard's lost 1948 manuscript. Amélie found the Corrections here, at 220 Hz.

carts.title: Basilica of Wires
carts.blurb: Down through the lettered levels to the subfloor the map won't print. The Order leaking into a second publisher's catalog.

carts.title: Northwind Harbor
carts.blurb: The dog, the seven mornings, the 1976 poster in the harbor-master's office. The first cart with the studio's name on it.

carts.title: Fey Faire
carts.blurb: A hundred and one feys at a traveling fairy carnival. Written while grieving a brother lost to one, in 1976.

carts.title: Riffmaster Melody Club
carts.credits: PDP Toys; synth by Marc Ostrom
carts.blurb: The kids' toy synth, made a game. There is one bar you only play once — the mourning bar.

carts.title: Hane no Niwa · The Feather Garden
carts.blurb: Keep the shrine, read the letters. The patience of a hanging scroll. Stay for the ninth sitting.

carts.title: Sweetgum
carts.blurb: Traded at zine tables, one ink. Camp Sweetgum's 1976 keeps rising to the waterline. Watch the island light.

carts.title: Estuary 1
carts.blurb: One lever, twelve weeks, the report card. No score, no fail state — the refusal that made RANCH walk out.

carts.title: Estuary 2
carts.credits: lead Marc Ostrom; species math Ines Rocha
carts.blurb: The coastline as arithmetic. The engine Pirate Summer borrowed backwards in time.

carts.title: Pirate Summer
carts.credits: Ines Rocha: four campers + Wilson Ashe
carts.blurb: Six days at Camp Sweetgum, 1988. The shortwave in the boathouse still carries 1976.

carts.title: Kwik Stop Manager
carts.blurb: The scoring layer Estuary 1 refused, made a whole game. SAM VS SAM begins here.

carts.title: Sam's Summer Shifts
carts.blurb: The KSM sequel. The other Sam's summer, the other side of the rivalry.

carts.title: Estuary 3
carts.credits: welded by Ines Rocha over a Labor Day weekend
carts.blurb: The summer at the Kwik Stop. Made in a weekend, then given a second Manager Mode summer. Year Two remembers year one.

carts.title: Mrs. Wu's Garden
carts.blurb: A Portland backyard, six evenings, frost triage. Made in stolen evenings the same October she welded Estuary 3.

carts.title: The Tideline
carts.blurb: Twelve stations, a two-line notebook, the seal, the 220 Hz buoy. Measurement as attention.

carts.title: Estuary 4
carts.blurb: The course-correction. Reads the register you filed in The Tideline and answers it.

carts.title: The Patient Mister Glass
carts.blurb: The evenings you let pass without a word. The second ledger, kept under the ledger.

carts.title: Tideline Survey
carts.credits: Meridian Heritage, on the licensed catalog
carts.blurb: The Tideline remade on auto-sensors — one surviving manual moment per license §4(c). Proof the 2004 original mattered.

achievements.title: First Cart
achievements.blurb: You played one of the shelf.

achievements.title: The House the Rochas Built
achievements.blurb: Played the Oneironautics run — the estuaries, end to end.

achievements.title: The Oldest Cart
achievements.blurb: You booted Sisters Wyrd (1983) — the oldest thing on the shelf.

achievements.title: Across the License
achievements.blurb: Played both Tidelines — the 2004 original and the 2048 remake.

achievements.title: The 220 Hz Publisher
achievements.blurb: Played both Astro-Cortex carts — Earthman and Basilica.
